questions.question: What does 'version control' primarily help you do?
questions.question_zh: 「版本控制」最主要的用途是什么？
options: Speed up code execution
Track changes to files over time and collaborate with others
Compress files to save disk space
Automatically fix bugs in your code
options_zh: 加速代码执行
追踪文件随时间的变化，并与他人协作
压缩文件以节省磁盘空间
自动修复代码中的 bug
questions.explanation: Version control systems like git track every change made to files, allowing you to revert to previous states and collaborate with others on the same codebase.
questions.explanation_zh: git 这类版本控制系统会记录每一次文件改动，让你可以回退到任意历史状态，也方便多人协作同一份代码库。

questions.question: What is a 'repository' in the context of software development?
questions.question_zh: 在软件开发语境下，「repository（仓库）」指的是什么？
options: A cloud storage service like Google Drive
A collection of files and their complete change history managed by version control
A database for storing user data
A package manager for installing libraries
options_zh: 类似 Google Drive 的云存储服务
由版本控制管理的一组文件及其完整变更历史
用来存放用户数据的数据库
用于安装库的包管理器
questions.explanation: A repository (repo) is a directory tracked by git that contains all project files along with the full history of changes made to those files.
questions.explanation_zh: repository（简称 repo）是被 git 跟踪的目录，里面既包含所有项目文件，也包含这些文件的完整变更历史。

questions.question: What is the correct sequence for saving and backing up your work in git?
questions.question_zh: 在 git 中保存并备份工作的正确顺序是什么？
options: git push, git add, git commit
git commit, git add, git push
git add, git commit, git push
git clone, git add, git push
options_zh: git push, git add, git commit
git commit, git add, git push
git add, git commit, git push
git clone, git add, git push
questions.explanation: First you stage changes with 'git add', then save a snapshot with 'git commit', then upload to the remote with 'git push'. This is the daily workflow.
questions.explanation_zh: 先用 git add 把改动加入暂存区，再用 git commit 拍快照，最后 git push 推到远端。这是日常工作流。

questions.question: What does 'git checkout -b experiment/new-optimizer' do?
questions.question_zh: git checkout -b experiment/new-optimizer 会做什么？
options: Downloads a branch from GitHub called experiment/new-optimizer
Creates a new branch named experiment/new-optimizer and switches to it
Deletes the branch experiment/new-optimizer
Reverts all files to match the experiment/new-optimizer branch
options_zh: 从 GitHub 下载名为 experiment/new-optimizer 的分支
创建一个名为 experiment/new-optimizer 的新分支并切换过去
删除 experiment/new-optimizer 分支
把所有文件回退到 experiment/new-optimizer 分支的状态
questions.explanation: The '-b' flag creates a new branch with the given name and immediately switches to it, allowing you to experiment without affecting the main branch.
questions.explanation_zh: -b 参数会以给定的名字创建新分支并立即切过去，这样你可以放心做实验，而不影响主分支。

questions.question: Why should you add '.pt', '.pth', and '.safetensors' to your .gitignore?
options: They are Python test files that should not be committed
They are model checkpoint files that are too large for git and can be re-generated
They contain sensitive API keys
Git cannot track binary files of any kind
options_zh: 它们是不应该提交的 Python 测试文件
它们是模型 checkpoint 文件，体积太大不适合 git，而且可以重新生成
它们包含敏感的 API key
git 完全无法跟踪任何二进制文件
questions.explanation: Model checkpoint files (.pt, .pth, .safetensors) are often gigabytes in size. Git is not designed for large binary files, so these should be excluded and re-generated or stored separately.
questions.explanation_zh: 模型 checkpoint 文件（.pt、.pth、.safetensors）动辄几个 GB。git 并不适合存大型二进制文件，所以应该把它们排除在外，需要时重新生成或单独存放。
